name: iSpace (Beijing Interstellar Glory Space Technology Co., Ltd.)

overview.value: i-Space (Chinese: Beijing Interstellar Glory Space Technology Co., Ltd.) is a Chinese private space technology development and space launch company based in Beijing, founded in October 2016. It operates the solid-propellant Hyperbola-1 launch vehicle and has conducted multiple orbital launch attempts since 2019.

focus.value: Space technology development and space launch company; developer/operator of the Hyperbola orbital launch vehicle family

notes: Added 2026-07-08 per Florian (Hyperbola-1 approval; parent org was absent). Identity verified as the Chinese launch company, distinct from ispace, Inc. of Tokyo, Japan (lunar landers). Slug ispace-china chosen so a future Japanese ispace profile cannot collide.

events.headline: iSpace's Hyperbola-1 reaches orbit on maiden flight
iSpace raises $171 million Series B funding round (per Payload)
iSpace's Hyperbola-1 fails on second orbital launch attempt
iSpace's Hyperbola-1 fails on third orbital launch attempt
iSpace's Hyperbola-1 fails on fourth orbital launch attempt
iSpace returns to flight with successful Hyperbola-1 launch
iSpace's Hyperbola-1 places DEAR-1 satellite in orbit
iSpace raises $99 million Series C and C+ funding round (per Payload)
